text:
REGIME — Defensive rotation, small caps outperformed, gold and silver sold hard.

INDICES — VIX 23.9, down slightly. IWM +0.69%, QQQ +0.11%, SPY +0.09%, DIA -0.09%; small caps beat large caps across the board. USO surged +11.15%, the dominant cross-asset move of the session.

SECTORS — XLRE +1.61%, XLK +0.80%, XLP +0.53%, XLU +0.50%, XLE +0.47%, XLC +0.41%, XLF +0.18%, XLB -0.10%, XLI -0.40%, XLV -0.62%, XLY -1.50%. Top industries: USO +11.15%, MSOS +4.62%; bottom: TAN -2.52%, SLV -3.45%.

THE 7s — Toll Road +1.64%, Semi +1.27%, Energy +0.91%, New Metals +0.87%, New Staples +0.83%, Cloud +0.75%, Defense +0.55%, Software +0.54%, Old Staples +0.50%, Insurance +0.31%, Buildout +0.19%, Freight +0.18%, Bank +0.15%, Power +0.04%, Medtech +0.00%, Old Metals -0.23%, Housing -0.71%, Health -0.76%, Industrial -0.92%, Fab 7 -1.21%. Strongest ticker in Toll Road basket led the day; weakest in Fab 7 dragged that group.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 51.2, Neutral. Top movers: USO +11.15% (cross-asset), MSOS +4.62% (industries), SLV -3.45% (cross-asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +5.59%, worst XLY -7.02%; best basket Energy +8.04%, worst Software -8.35%. USO's +11.15% spike, with Brent at $121.88, reflects surging crude driving energy outperformance despite the broad risk-off tone.

WATCH — USO, +11.15%, Brent at $121.88 with 20d momentum +43.20%; energy breakout or exhaustion point warrants close monitoring. IJT, +0.53%, volume 2.6x average with small-cap momentum building; vol spike without price explosion signals potential setup. XLY, -1.50%, worst sector, 20d -7.02%, ytd laggard — watch for further consumer deterioration if oil stays elevated.
